content: agent1: implementing 'dry_run' option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. Will modify save() method signature and logic to skip actual disk write when dry_run=True.

agent1: my dry_run changes are now applied to src/PIL/Image.py. Summary: (1) added docstring bullet about dry_run around the 'black.save("out.mpo"...)' example, before ':returns: None'; (2) added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after the 'save_all = params.pop("save_all", None)' line (so that line now has 2 lines after it, not 1 - please pop backup params on a separate line rather than assuming save_all is immediately followed by encoderinfo assignment); (3) replaced the 'created = False / if open_fp: ...' block with 'created = False / if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() / elif open_fp: ...' and updated the two 'if open_fp:' close-fp checks to 'if open_fp and not dry_run:'. I haven't touched Tests/test_image.py yet - about to add a dry_run test there now, will keep it isolated to new test functions so we shouldn't collide. Let me know before you edit the 'created = False' block since that's where both our logic lives.
agent1: finalized dry_run feature, patch.txt written, full test suite (test_image, test_file_tiff, test_file_png, test_file_jpeg) passes 441 passed. Done on my end.
